steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim says My Name Is Red ‘resembles classic magical realist novels in its juxtaposition of literary realism with folklore — namely, that of Turkey.’ The passage defines magical realism as antirealistic, folkloric devices ‘deployed in an otherwise realistic mode.’ The key mechanism is folklore disrupting a realistic framework from within. The correct quotation must directly support this juxtaposition — realism as the dominant mode, interrupted by folkloric elements.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Like many works in the Latin American magical realist tradition, *My Name Is Red* is indebted to antirealistic elements in the folkloric tradition of Turkey.": Says the novel is ‘indebted to’ folklore as an influence, but does not describe folklore disrupting a realistic mode. No juxtaposition mechanism is identified. ✗
  • Choice B — "Although Turkish folklore clearly informs the style and occasionally antirealistic plot of *My Name Is Red*, the novel also shows the inarguable influence of the magical realist tradition of Latin America.": Focuses on the novel's debt to the Latin American tradition, not on how folklore interacts with realism within the novel itself. ✗
  • Choice C — "While *My Name Is Red* alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes of representation, details suggesting the influence of Turkish folklore nevertheless occur throughout the novel.": Describes alternation between modes, which differs from magical realism's structure (folklore embedded inside an otherwise realistic mode, not swapping between them). ✗
  • Choice D — "The logic of the realistic plot of *My Name Is Red* is repeatedly and productively disrupted by the presence of imagery and situations drawn from Turkish folklore.": Directly mirrors the definition: a realistic mode disrupted from within by folkloric elements. ‘Repeatedly and productively disrupted’ captures the juxtaposition the underlined claim describes. ✓
That matches Choice D — "The logic of the realistic plot of *My Name Is Red* is repeatedly and productively disrupted by the presence of imagery and situations drawn from Turkish folklore.".
